days.title: Introduction
days.headline: The 48 Laws of Power
days.body: Power is a game. The skillful player observes, plans, and acts with patience. Over the next 48 days, we walk through each law — not to manipulate, but to recognize the moves being played around us, and to walk through life with our eyes open.

days.title: Law 1
days.headline: Never Outshine the Master
days.body: Always make those above you feel comfortably superior. In your desire to please and impress them, do not go too far in displaying your talents, or you might accomplish the opposite — inspire fear and insecurity.

days.title: Law 2
days.headline: Never Put Too Much Trust in Friends; Learn How to Use Enemies
days.body: Be wary of friends — they will betray you more quickly, for they are easily aroused to envy. Hire a former enemy and they will be more loyal than a friend, because they have more to prove.

days.title: Law 3
days.headline: Conceal Your Intentions
days.body: Keep people off-balance and in the dark by never revealing the purpose behind your actions. If they have no clue what you are up to, they cannot prepare a defense.

days.title: Law 4
days.headline: Always Say Less Than Necessary
days.body: When you are trying to impress people with words, the more you say, the more common you appear, and the less in control. Powerful people impress and intimidate by saying less.

days.title: Law 5
days.headline: So Much Depends on Reputation — Guard It With Your Life
days.body: Reputation is the cornerstone of power. Through reputation alone you can intimidate and win. Once it slips, however, you are vulnerable and will be attacked on all sides.

days.title: Law 6
days.headline: Court Attention at All Costs
days.body: Everything is judged by its appearance; what is unseen counts for nothing. Never let yourself get lost in the crowd. Be conspicuous at all costs. Make yourself a magnet of attention.

days.title: Law 7
days.headline: Get Others to Do the Work for You, but Always Take the Credit
days.body: Use the wisdom, knowledge, and legwork of other people to further your own cause. Such assistance saves you valuable time and energy and gives you a godlike aura of efficiency and speed.

days.title: Law 8
days.headline: Make Other People Come to You — Use Bait if Necessary
days.body: When you force the other person to act, you are the one in control. It is always better to make your opponent come to you, abandoning his own plans in the process. Lure him with fabulous gains, then attack.

days.title: Law 9
days.headline: Win Through Your Actions, Never Through Argument
days.body: Any momentary triumph you think you have gained through argument is really a Pyrrhic victory: the resentment and ill will you stir up is stronger and lasts longer than any momentary change of opinion. Demonstrate, do not explicate.

days.title: Law 10
days.headline: Infection: Avoid the Unhappy and Unlucky
days.body: You can die from someone else's misery — emotional states are as infectious as diseases. The unfortunate sometimes draw misfortune on themselves; they will also draw it on you. Associate with the happy and fortunate instead.

days.title: Law 11
days.headline: Learn to Keep People Dependent on You
days.body: To maintain your independence you must always be needed and wanted. The more you are relied on, the more freedom you have. Make people depend on you for their happiness and prosperity, and you have nothing to fear.

days.title: Law 12
days.headline: Use Selective Honesty and Generosity to Disarm Your Victim
days.body: One sincere and honest move will cover over dozens of dishonest ones. Open-hearted gestures of honesty and generosity bring down the guard of even the most suspicious people.

days.title: Law 13
days.headline: When Asking for Help, Appeal to Self-Interest, Never to Mercy or Gratitude
days.body: If you need to turn to an ally for help, do not bother to remind him of your past assistance and good deeds. He will find a way to ignore you. Instead, uncover something in your request that will benefit him, and emphasize it out of all proportion.

days.title: Law 14
days.headline: Pose as a Friend, Work as a Spy
days.body: Knowing about your rival is critical. Use spies to gather valuable information that will keep you a step ahead. Better still: play the spy yourself. In polite social encounters, learn to probe — ask indirect questions to get people to reveal their weaknesses and intentions.

days.title: Law 15
days.headline: Crush Your Enemy Totally
days.body: More is lost through stopping halfway than through total annihilation: the enemy will recover, and will seek revenge. Crush him, not only in body but in spirit.

days.title: Law 16
days.headline: Use Absence to Increase Respect and Honor
days.body: Too much circulation makes the price go down: the more you are seen and heard from, the more common you appear. Withdraw for a while, and you will be talked about, even admired. You must learn when to leave.

days.title: Law 17
days.headline: Keep Others in Suspended Terror: Cultivate Unpredictability
days.body: Humans are creatures of habit with an insatiable need to see familiarity in others' actions. Your predictability gives them a sense of control. Turn the tables: be deliberately unpredictable. Behavior that seems to have no consistency or purpose will keep them off-balance.

days.title: Law 18
days.headline: Do Not Build Fortresses to Protect Yourself — Isolation Is Dangerous
days.body: The world is dangerous and enemies are everywhere — everyone has to protect themselves. A fortress seems the safest. But isolation exposes you to more dangers than it protects you from — it cuts you off from valuable information, it makes you conspicuous and an easy target. Better to circulate among people.

days.title: Law 19
days.headline: Know Who You're Dealing With — Do Not Offend the Wrong Person
days.body: There are many different kinds of people in the world, and you can never assume that everyone will react to your strategies in the same way. Deceive or outmaneuver some people and they will spend the rest of their lives seeking revenge. Choose your victims and opponents carefully.

days.title: Law 20
days.headline: Do Not Commit to Anyone
days.body: It is the fool who always rushes to take sides. Do not commit to any side or cause but yourself. By maintaining your independence, you become the master of others — playing people against one another, making them pursue you.

days.title: Law 21
days.headline: Play a Sucker to Catch a Sucker — Seem Dumber Than Your Mark
days.body: No one likes feeling stupider than the next person. The trick is to make your victims feel smart — and not just smart, but smarter than you are. Once convinced of this, they will never suspect that you may have ulterior motives.

days.title: Law 22
days.headline: Use the Surrender Tactic: Transform Weakness Into Power
days.body: When you are weaker, never fight for honor's sake; choose surrender instead. Surrender gives you time to recover, time to torment and irritate your conqueror, time to wait for his power to wane. Turn the cheek and infuriate him.

days.title: Law 23
days.headline: Concentrate Your Forces
days.body: Conserve your forces and energies by keeping them concentrated at their strongest point. You gain more by finding a rich mine and mining it deeper than by flitting from one shallow mine to another — intensity defeats extensity every time.

days.title: Law 24
days.headline: Play the Perfect Courtier
days.body: The perfect courtier thrives in a world where everything revolves around power and political dexterity. He has mastered the art of indirection; he flatters, yields to superiors, and asserts power over others in the most oblique and graceful manner.

days.title: Law 25
days.headline: Re-Create Yourself
days.body: Do not accept the roles that society foists on you. Re-create yourself by forging a new identity, one that commands attention and never bores the audience. Be the master of your own image rather than letting others define it for you.

days.title: Law 26
days.headline: Keep Your Hands Clean
days.body: You must seem a paragon of civility and efficiency: your hands are never soiled by mistakes and nasty deeds. Maintain such a spotless appearance by using others as scapegoats and cat's-paws to disguise your involvement.

days.title: Law 27
days.headline: Play on People's Need to Believe to Create a Cultlike Following
days.body: People have an overwhelming desire to believe in something. Become the focal point of such desire by offering them a cause, a new faith to follow. Promise the moon but keep your words vague yet full of promise; emphasize enthusiasm over rationality.

days.title: Law 28
days.headline: Enter Action With Boldness
days.body: If you are unsure of a course of action, do not attempt it. Your doubts and hesitations will infect your execution. Timidity is dangerous: better to enter with boldness. Any mistakes you commit through audacity are easily corrected with more audacity.

days.title: Law 29
days.headline: Plan All the Way to the End
days.body: The ending is everything. Plan all the way to it, taking into account all the possible consequences, obstacles, and twists of fortune that might reverse your hard work. By planning to the end, you will not be overwhelmed by circumstances.

days.title: Law 30
days.headline: Make Your Accomplishments Seem Effortless
days.body: Your actions must seem natural and executed with ease. All the toil and practice that go into them, and also all the clever tricks, must be concealed. When you act, act effortlessly, as if you could do much more.

days.title: Law 31
days.headline: Control the Options: Get Others to Play With the Cards You Deal
days.body: The best deceptions are the ones that seem to give the other person a choice: your victims feel they are in control, but are actually your puppets. Give people options that come out in your favor whichever one they choose.

days.title: Law 32
days.headline: Play to People's Fantasies
days.body: The truth is often avoided because it is ugly and unpleasant. Never appeal to truth and reality unless you are prepared for the anger that comes from disenchantment. Life is so harsh and distressing that those who can manufacture romance or conjure up fantasy are like oases in the desert.

days.title: Law 33
days.headline: Discover Each Man's Thumbscrew
days.body: Everyone has a weakness, a gap in the castle wall. That weakness is usually an insecurity, an uncontrollable emotion or need. Once found, it is a thumbscrew you can turn to your advantage.

days.title: Law 34
days.headline: Be Royal in Your Own Fashion: Act Like a King to Be Treated Like One
days.body: The way you carry yourself will often determine how you are treated: in the long run, appearing vulgar or common will make people disrespect you. A king respects himself and inspires the same sentiment in others.

days.title: Law 35
days.headline: Master the Art of Timing
days.body: Never seem to be in a hurry — hurrying betrays a lack of control over yourself, and over time. Always seem patient, as if you know that everything will come to you eventually. Become a detective of the right moment.

days.title: Law 36
days.headline: Disdain Things You Cannot Have: Ignoring Them Is the Best Revenge
days.body: By acknowledging a petty problem you give it existence and credibility. The more attention you pay an enemy, the stronger you make him; and a small mistake is often made worse and more visible when you try to fix it.

days.title: Law 37
days.headline: Create Compelling Spectacles
days.body: Striking imagery and grand symbolic gestures create the aura of power — everyone responds to them. Stage spectacles for those around you, then, full of arresting visuals and radiant symbols that heighten your presence.

days.title: Law 38
days.headline: Think as You Like but Behave Like Others
days.body: If you make a show of going against the times, flaunting your unconventional ideas and unorthodox ways, people will think that you only want attention and that you look down upon them. They will find a way to punish you. Far safer to blend in and nurture the common touch.

days.title: Law 39
days.headline: Stir Up Waters to Catch Fish
days.body: Anger and emotion are strategically counterproductive. You must always stay calm and objective. But if you can make your enemies angry while staying calm yourself, you gain a decided advantage. Put your enemies off-balance: find the chink in their vanity.

days.title: Law 40
days.headline: Despise the Free Lunch
days.body: What is offered for free is dangerous — it usually involves either a trick or a hidden obligation. What has worth is worth paying for. By paying your own way you stay clear of gratitude, guilt, and deceit.

days.title: Law 41
days.headline: Avoid Stepping Into a Great Man's Shoes
days.body: What happens first always appears better and more original than what comes after. If you succeed a great man or have a famous parent, you will have to accomplish double their achievements to outshine them. Do not get lost in their shadow — establish your own name.

days.title: Law 42
days.headline: Strike the Shepherd and the Sheep Will Scatter
days.body: Trouble can often be traced to a single strong individual — the stirrer, the arrogant underling, the poisoner of goodwill. If you allow such people room to operate, others will succumb to their influence. Do not wait for the troubles they cause to multiply; strike at their source.

days.title: Law 43
days.headline: Work on the Hearts and Minds of Others
days.body: Coercion creates a reaction that will eventually work against you. You must seduce others into wanting to move in your direction. The way to seduce others is to operate on their individual psychologies and weaknesses. Soften up the resistant by working on their emotions.

days.title: Law 44
days.headline: Disarm and Infuriate With the Mirror Effect
days.body: The mirror reflects reality, but it is also the perfect tool for deception. When you mirror your enemies, doing exactly as they do, they cannot figure out your strategy. The mirror mocks and humiliates them, making them overreact.

days.title: Law 45
days.headline: Preach the Need for Change, but Never Reform Too Much at Once
days.body: Everyone understands the need for change in the abstract, but on the day-to-day level people are creatures of habit. Too much innovation is traumatic, and will lead to revolt. If you are new to a position of power, show that you respect the old ways.

days.title: Law 46
days.headline: Never Appear Too Perfect
days.body: Appearing better than others is always dangerous, but most dangerous of all is to appear to have no faults or weaknesses. Envy creates silent enemies. It is smart to occasionally display defects, and admit to harmless vices, to deflect envy and appear more human and approachable.

days.title: Law 47
days.headline: Do Not Go Past the Mark You Aimed For; In Victory, Learn When to Stop
days.body: The moment of victory is often the moment of greatest peril. In the heat of victory, arrogance and overconfidence can push you past the goal you had aimed for, and by going too far, you make more enemies than you defeat. Set a goal, and when you reach it, stop.

days.title: Law 48
days.headline: Assume Formlessness
days.body: By taking a shape, by having a visible plan, you open yourself to attack. Instead of taking a form for your enemy to grasp, keep yourself adaptable and on the move. Accept that nothing is certain and no law is fixed. The best way to protect yourself is to be as fluid and formless as water.
